note_data.title: Letter Writing (خط نگاری)
keyPoints: Cambridge O Level First Language Urdu 3247 is available for exams in 2025 and 2026
Exams are available in the June series only
The syllabus consists of Paper 1 Reading and Writing and Paper 2 Texts
Khatoot o Ghalib by Mirza Ghalib is included as a set text in Part 3 Prose
The syllabus has been updated to version 2, published November 2022
Cambridge O Level is internationally recognised as equivalent to Cambridge IGCSE

definitions.term: Cambridge O Level First Language Urdu 3247
definitions.definition: An international qualification that prepares students for life by developing informed curiosity and lasting passion for learning in Urdu language

howToAnswer.instruction: Follow the exact format for the letter type specified. Include all required elements: date, address, greeting, body, closing.
Create a complete letter with proper Urdu expressions and maintain consistent tone throughout

modelAnswer.question: اپنے دوست کو عید کی دعوت کا خط لکھیں۔ (Write a letter to your friend inviting him for Eid celebration)
modelAnswer.answer:
کراچی
15 اپریل 2024

عزیز دوست احمد،
السلام علیکم

امید ہے آپ خیریت سے ہوں گے۔ عید الفطر کا مبارک موقع قریب آ رہا ہے اور میں آپ کو اپنے گھر عید منانے کی دعوت دینا چاہتا ہوں۔

عید کا دن ہمارے یہاں بہت خوشی سے منایا جائے گا۔ صبح نماز عید کے بعد دوپہر میں کھانا ہوگا۔ آپ اپنے اہل خانہ کے ساتھ تشریف لائیں۔

برائے کرم 20 اپریل تک بتا دیں کہ آپ آ سکیں گے یا نہیں تاکہ تیاری مکمل ہو سکے۔

آپ کے جواب کا انتظار ہے۔

آپ کا دوست
علی
markBreakdown: Mark 1-2: Correct format with date and address
Mark 5-10: Content - invitation details, event information
Mark 14-15: Overall coherence and cultural appropriateness

commonMistakes: Using outdated syllabus versions instead of the current version 2 published November 2022
Not checking if the syllabus is available in your specific administrative zone
Assuming exams are available in multiple series when they are only offered in June
examinerTips: Read the whole syllabus before planning your teaching programme
Check the syllabus page at www.cambridgeinternational.org/olevel to verify availability in your administrative zone
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Standard letter format diagram showing placement of: date (top right), address (sender's), salutation (left aligned), body paragraphs (justified), closing phrase (right aligned), and signature (bottom right)
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q1 (8 marks) — they asked students to write a formal letter to the principal requesting permission for a school trip, including proper format and persuasive language
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P2 Q2 (6 marks) — students had to write an informal letter to a friend describing a recent festival celebration, focusing on descriptive language and personal tone
This topic was examined in M/J 2022 P2 Q3 (8 marks) — candidates wrote a complaint letter to a local newspaper about traffic problems, requiring formal tone and structured arguments
This topic was tested in O/N 2021 P2 Q1 (6 marks) — students wrote a letter of invitation to relatives for a wedding ceremony, emphasizing cultural elements and warm tone

workedExample.question: Write a letter to your friend describing your experience of visiting a historical monument. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Use informal letter format - your address (top right), date, 'Dear [friend's name]', friendly greeting
Step 2: Opening paragraph - explain purpose of letter and briefly mention the monument visit
Step 3: Body paragraphs - describe the monument's appearance, historical significance, your feelings, interesting incidents
Step 4: Use descriptive language, personal opinions, and engaging details to make it lively
Step 5: Closing paragraph - summarize experience and invite friend to visit too
Step 6: End with appropriate closing ('Yours lovingly', 'Best wishes') and your name
workedExample.answer:
123 Model Street
Karachi
15th March 2024

Dear Sarah,

I hope you are in good health. I am writing to share my amazing experience of visiting Mohenjo-Daro last weekend.

The ancient ruins were absolutely breathtaking! Walking through the well-planned streets of this 4000-year-old civilization felt like traveling back in time. The Great Bath and the sophisticated drainage system amazed me. Our guide explained how advanced the Indus Valley people were in urban planning.

What struck me most was the peaceful atmosphere and the mystery surrounding this lost civilization. I felt so proud of our rich heritage. The museum displayed beautiful pottery and seals that showed their artistic skills.

You must visit this incredible site sometime. I'm sure you'll love exploring our ancient history as much as I did.

Yours lovingly,
Ali
workedExample.markScheme: Content and ideas (3 marks) - relevant details about monument visit, personal experience. Language and style (2 marks) - appropriate informal tone, varied vocabulary. Format (1 mark) - correct letter structure with all components

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates failed to maintain consistent tone throughout their letters' — always ensure formal letters stay formal and informal letters maintain friendly tone from start to finish

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often mixed up the format requirements for formal and informal letters' — remember formal letters need full addresses of both sender and receiver, while informal letters only need sender's address

Examiner Report 2021: 'Candidates frequently wrote too briefly and failed to develop their ideas adequately' — always expand your points with examples, details, and explanations to meet the word count and content requirements
note_data.keyDiagram: Letter Format Structure Diagram: Draw a rectangular frame showing proper letter layout - sender's address (top right corner), date (below address), receiver's address (left side for formal letters), salutation (Dear Sir/Madam or Dear [name]), body paragraphs with clear spacing, complimentary close (Yours faithfully/sincerely for formal, Yours lovingly/Best wishes for informal), and signature with printed name. Mark margins and proper spacing between sections.
note_data.quickRecap: Cambridge O Level First Language Urdu 3247 is an internationally recognised qualification available for 2025 and 2026 examinations in June series only. The assessment includes Paper 1 Reading and Writing and Paper 2 Texts, with Khatoot o Ghalib by Mirza Ghalib as a set text. Teachers should use the updated version 2 syllabus published in November 2022.
